task: Offer ~10 preset status icons to pick from when creating a tag, instead of an unassisted free-text emoji field.

evidence.current_icon_input: settings.component.html:162-166 — plain <input maxlength="4" placeholder="🔥">, no picker. The colour field beside it IS a native picker (:174), so icon is the only unassisted field of the three.
evidence.storage: Tags.icon is TEXT NOT NULL, no length constraint, no CHECK (DatabaseInitializer.cs:133-139, schema v7). Entity: Entities.cs:113-114. Unchanged by this task.
evidence.no_api_change_needed: TagDto / SettingsTagCreateRequest / SettingsTagUpdateRequest already carry Icon as string (Dtos.cs:107, SettingsEndpoints.cs:1185,1187). A preset picker writes the same field — no contract change, no client regen, no migration.
evidence.no_existing_preset_list: Searched for ICONS/EMOJI/PRESET/PALETTE arrays across all non-node_modules source: none exists for tag icons. Nearest neighbours are TYPE_COLORS and AVATAR_COLORS (worklog.service.ts:189-201) and a single default tag colour (settings.component.ts:28).
evidence.render_sites_unaffected: Icons render as literal interpolated glyphs at tag-picker.component.html:40-42, task-list.component.html:216, settings.component.html:187. A picker changes what gets stored, not how it renders.
evidence.test_baseline: No test asserts anything about icon content, length or validation anywhere. The maxlength="4" cap is entirely untested. Two real icon assertions exist and both only check the glyph passes through to render (tag-picker.component.spec.ts:77, task-list.model.spec.ts:264-266).

reason: Purely additive and client-side with no contract or schema impact, but it is a NEW FEATURE with a user-facing behaviour change — an explicit hard exclusion in STEP 1 — and the two genuinely open questions (which 10 glyphs, and whether the picker replaces or supplements free text) are product decisions no agent should settle alone.
open_questions_for_brainstorm: Which 10 glyphs? The user said 'commonly used to describe status in reports' but did not name them — this is a product choice with no oracle in the repo.

Does the picker REPLACE the free-text input or sit beside it? Replacing caps icons at the preset set (a real loss for anyone already using a custom glyph); supplementing keeps the uncapped free-text path alive.
Existing tags carry arbitrary glyphs already. Presets must not orphan or rewrite them.
adjacent_findings_not_in_scope: No CSS sets an emoji font-family anywhere (only body/mono at styles.scss:53,61,114), despite tag-picker.component.html:37-38 stating icons are emoji meant to match WPF's Segoe UI Emoji. Rendering currently relies on system fallback.
maxlength="4" is a browser-only cap; the API validates only Text (SettingsEndpoints.cs:176-177, :193-194), so an arbitrarily long icon is accepted server-side and an empty icon is valid end-to-end.
settings.component.html:187 interpolates '{{ t.icon }} {{ t.text }}' without the @if guard the other two render sites have, so an empty icon leaves a leading space.
fallback_path: If brainstorm settles the two open questions and the change stays inside the Settings page with no contract impact, this becomes a single small plan at STEP 6 — not a multi-wave milestone.
